nodes.label: Onboarding Slide 01 Hero — Live Commerce Platform on Laptop

nodes.label: Brand Identity Pattern (BrandMark / BrandWordmark / LiventoLogo duplication)
nodes.norm_label: brand identity pattern (brandmark / brandwordmark / liventologo duplication)

nodes.label: Server Component First Pattern
nodes.rationale: Default to Server Components; add 'use client' only when state, effects, event handlers, or browser APIs are needed. This minimizes client JS bundle size.

nodes.label: Tailwind v4 CSS-first Configuration
nodes.rationale: All design tokens go in globals.css via @theme, not in tailwind.config.js which does not exist in v4. @import 'tailwindcss' replaces @tailwind directives.

nodes.label: Next.js 16 params as Promise
nodes.rationale: In Next.js 16, params and searchParams are Promises and must be awaited. Destructuring them directly as in v13/14 will throw at runtime.

nodes.label: Component Colocation Rule
nodes.rationale: Components used by one route go in app/route/_components/. Components shared by 2+ routes go in components/. Do not pre-emptively move to components/.

nodes.label: Frontend Agent — Next.js 16 + React 19 + Tailwind v4
nodes.norm_label: frontend agent — next.js 16 + react 19 + tailwind v4

nodes.label: Next.js 16
nodes.rationale: App Router only; Pages Router is legacy. Breaking changes: params/searchParams are Promises (not plain objects), fetch is NOT cached by default (changed from v13/14).

nodes.label: React 19
nodes.rationale: Supports Server Functions, use() hook, and form actions.

nodes.label: Tailwind CSS v4
nodes.rationale: CSS-first configuration. No tailwind.config.js. All config via @theme in globals.css. PostCSS plugin is @tailwindcss/postcss.

nodes.label: TypeScript 5
nodes.rationale: Strict mode enabled. No use of any type — use unknown and narrow instead.

nodes.label: params/searchParams as Promises (Next.js 16 breaking change)
nodes.rationale: In Next.js 16, params and searchParams passed to page components are Promises, not plain objects. Must be awaited before destructuring.

nodes.label: fetch not cached by default (Next.js 16 breaking change)
nodes.rationale: Unlike Next.js 13/14, fetch responses are NOT cached by default in Next.js 16. Must opt in via cache: force-cache or the use cache directive.
nodes.norm_label: fetch not cached by default (next.js 16 breaking change)

nodes.label: use cache directive
nodes.rationale: Next.js 16 directive for opting into caching on a per-function basis. Used for stable data like product catalogs.

nodes.label: @theme directive
nodes.rationale: Tailwind v4 mechanism for defining design tokens in CSS. CSS variables in @theme become Tailwind utility classes automatically.

nodes.label: Server Component
nodes.rationale: Default component type in Next.js App Router. Used for data fetching, DB/API access, secret access, and reducing client JS bundle size.

nodes.label: Client Component
nodes.rationale: Components marked with 'use client'. Required for useState, useReducer, useEffect, event handlers, browser APIs, and third-party React hooks. Push as deep in the tree as possible to minimize client bundle.

nodes.label: use client directive
nodes.rationale: Must be added only when a component needs browser-only APIs, React state, effects, or event handlers. Should be pushed as deep in the component tree as possible.

nodes.label: server-only module
nodes.rationale: Import server-only in server-side modules to cause a build error if accidentally imported in a Client Component. Prevents server code from leaking to the client.

nodes.label: App Router
nodes.rationale: Next.js App Router is the only supported routing mechanism. Pages Router is legacy and should not be used.

nodes.label: Route Group
nodes.rationale: Folder prefixed with (name) groups routes under a shared layout without affecting the URL segment.

nodes.label: Private Folder (_components)
nodes.rationale: Folders prefixed with _ are not treated as route segments. Safe for colocating non-page files like components.

nodes.label: App Router File Conventions
nodes.rationale: layout.tsx, page.tsx, loading.tsx, error.tsx (must be use client), not-found.tsx, route.ts, template.tsx. Render hierarchy: layout → template → error → loading → not-found → page.

nodes.label: Server Functions (use server)
nodes.rationale: Marked with 'use server'. Used for mutations. Always verify auth before mutating. Call revalidatePath to invalidate cache. Can be called from Client Components via form actions.

nodes.label: Suspense Streaming Pattern
nodes.rationale: Wrap async Server Components in Suspense with a fallback skeleton to avoid blocking the full page render during data fetching.

nodes.label: TypeScript Rules
nodes.rationale: strict: true required. No any — use unknown and narrow. Export props types alongside components using ComponentNameProps naming pattern.

nodes.label: Performance Rules
nodes.rationale: Server Components first; use next/image, next/link, next/font; lazy-load heavy Client Components with dynamic(); wrap async data in Suspense; use cache for stable data.

nodes.label: Naming Conventions
nodes.rationale: Route files: lowercase. Components: PascalCase. Hooks: use prefix + camelCase. Utilities: camelCase. Server actions: camelCase. Types/interfaces: PascalCase. CSS vars: --kebab-case.

nodes.label: Three-Layer Styling Pattern
nodes.rationale: Layer 1: Tailwind for layout/spacing/typography/grid. Layer 2: globals.css semantic classes for complex visual effects (gradients, shadows, animations). Layer 3: inline style ONLY for dynamic runtime values. Never inline styles for static layout. Never Tailwind arbitrary values for complex gradients — use named globals.css classes.

nodes.label: Live Commerce UI Patterns
nodes.rationale: Live offers need countdown timers (use client + useEffect). Real-time stock uses WebSocket/SSE (use client). Product stream feed is Server Component. Cart state is client-side context. Payment flows use Server Functions (never expose keys to client).

nodes.label: LiveStream.tsx
nodes.rationale: Client Component — requires browser APIs for video/WebRTC.

nodes.label: OfferCountdown.tsx
nodes.rationale: Client Component — requires timer state management.

nodes.label: LiveChat.tsx
nodes.rationale: Client Component — requires real-time communication.

nodes.label: ProductShowcase.tsx
nodes.rationale: Server Component — fetches product data server-side.

nodes.label: graphify Knowledge Graph Navigation
nodes.rationale: Mandatory codebase navigation via graphify-out/. Use graphify query, explain, path commands instead of reading files directly. Fall back to Read/grep only for files not yet indexed.

nodes.label: Context Provider Pattern
nodes.rationale: Context providers must be Client Components. Server Components (like RootLayout) wrap them as children so the provider is isolated and the layout itself remains a Server Component.

nodes.label: Store Conditional Render (store exists vs welcome)
nodes.norm_label: store conditional render (store exists vs welcome)

nodes.label: Server Component by default — add use client only when needed
nodes.norm_label: server component by default — add use client only when needed

nodes.norm_label: push use client as deep as possible

nodes.label: Colocation rule — one route: colocate; two+ routes: components/
nodes.norm_label: colocation rule — one route: colocate; two+ routes: components/

nodes.label: Styling pattern — three layers: Tailwind, globals.css, inline style
nodes.norm_label: styling pattern — three layers: tailwind, globals.css, inline style

nodes.norm_label: bottom nav must use react portal — screen-enter creates stacking context
